title: Array (ARRY) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text:
Array Technologies Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary

Key Financial Metrics:

Revenue & Profitability:
- Q1 revenue: $223 million (flat QoQ despite 15% volume increase due to lower average selling prices)
- Adjusted gross margin: 30.7% (up 620 basis points sequentially)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $29 million (up 157% sequentially)
- Adjusted EPS: $0.06 vs. loss of $0.01 in Q4

Order Book & Backlog:
- Record order book: $2.4 billion
- Book-to-bill ratio: ~2x for the quarter
- 12-month trailing book-to-bill: 1.3x
- ~80% expected to convert over next 6 quarters
- >95% domestic mix; ~50% from Tier 1 customers

Strategic Highlights:

Product Innovation:
- Over 50% of order book comprises products launched within past two years
- OmniTrack backlog exceeded DuraTrack for the first time
- DuraTrack D2S formal launch scheduled for Intersolar Munich in June
- New product features passive wind stow technology (up to 4% energy yield improvement)

International Expansion:
- New contracts executed in Turkey, Peru, and Colombia
- Targeting markets with challenging terrain or local content requirements
- APA order book reached ~$150 million (50% sequential increase)

Financial Position:
- Total liquidity: $550 million ($200M cash + $370M undrawn revolver)
- Net debt leverage: 2.7x trailing EBITDA
- Capital expenditures: $7.5 million (manufacturing capabilities)

2026 Guidance (Reaffirmed):
- Full-year adjusted gross margin: 26%-27%
- Q2 revenue outlook: $300-320 million
- Q2 adjusted gross margin: higher end of full-year range
- Company expects to achieve cash generation in 2026

Key Risks & Challenges:
CEO Kevin Hostetler noted: We absorb elevated logistics costs and don't change guidance one quarter after giving it. Challenges include:
- Elevated logistics costs from Middle East conflict
- Macroeconomic pressures in Spain and Brazil
- Seasonal and project-timing revenue variability
